scaled_oracle_cases.description: Comprehensive test covering all scaled requirements: multi-separator, unicode, consecutive separators, only separators, trailing separator, exactly two fields, and separator in prefix.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly extracts the last two fields from read IDs under all specified edge cases and data variants, including Unicode and empty fields.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must split on the given separator, return (UMI_bytes, cell_barcode_bytes) as bytes, raise ValueError if fewer than two separators, and handle empty fields, Unicode, and separator appearing in prefix.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A naive solution that assumes exactly two separators, non-empty fields, or ASCII-only would fail one or more of these cases.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
read_id_good = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001_CB10123_UMIACTGGA'
read_id_bad = 'NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001'
sep = '_'
sep_unicode = ':'
read_id_multi = 'prefix1_prefix2_CB_UMI'
read_id_unicode = 'run:flowcell:lane:tile:CB_\u00b5UMI:\u03b4'
read_id_consec = 'prefix__CB_UMI'
read_id_onlysep = '___'
read_id_trail = 'prefix_CB_'
read_id_two = 'CB_UMI'
read_id_adv = 'UMI_CB_UMI'
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_good, sep)
print("Good read -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)
try:
    get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_bad, sep)
except ValueError as e:
    print("Expected error for bad read ID:", e)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_multi, sep)
print("Multi-sep -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_unicode, sep_unicode)
print("Unicode -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_consec, sep)
print("Consecutive -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_onlysep, sep)
print("Only separators -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_trail, sep)
print("Trailing -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_two, sep)
print("Two fields -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

umi, cell_barcode = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id_adv, sep)
print("Separator in prefix -> UMI bytes:", umi, "Cell barcode bytes:", cell_barcode)

result = {'tested': True}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a multi-separator read ID.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with Unicode characters.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with consecutive separators.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID consisting solely of separator characters.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with a trailing separator.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with exactly two fields.
Scaled executable cases must include a read ID where the separator appears in the prefix (e.g., 'UMI_CB_UMI').

stdout_contains: Good read -> UMI bytes: b'UMIACTGGA' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB10123'
Expected error for bad read ID: Could not extract UMI or CB from the read ID, pleasecheck UMI and CB are encoded in the read name:NB551068:51:H3JTYBGXX:1:11101:10000:1001
Multi-sep -> UMI bytes: b'UMI' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB'
Unicode -> UMI bytes: b'\xce\xb4' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB_\xc2\xb5UMI'
Consecutive -> UMI bytes: b'UMI' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB'
Only separators -> UMI bytes: b'' Cell barcode bytes: b''
Trailing -> UMI bytes: b'' Cell barcode bytes: b'CB'
